question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() logs the full error on connection failure (log.Warn(err)) but does not halt execution, causing subsequent calls to db.DB() on a nil gorm.DB to return an error that is also only logged, resulting in a nil DbClient being returned and fee queries silently returning zero values?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool MaxOpenConnections limit in ConnectToDb() by sending a burst of fee-query requests that saturate the pool, causing subsequent fee queries to queue and eventually time out, returning default zero ChargedTxFee values to API consumers outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the context.WithTimeout() call in GetDbWithContext() by sending requests with already-expired contexts, causing the child context to be immediately cancelled and fee queries to return empty result sets, which may be interpreted as zero ChargedTxFee by upstream callers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the time.Duration(dbConfig.Pool.MaxLifetime) * time.Minute conversion in ConnectToDb() with a MaxLifetime of 0, causing connections to never expire and always serve the same DB snapshot, preventing fee schedule updates from being reflected in ChargedTxFee queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not enforce read-only mode on the returned DbClient, allowing any component that receives the DbClient to execute write queries that modify ChargedTxFee or MaxFee values in the transaction table outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of query parameter sanitization in GetDsn() (used by ConnectToDb()) to inject additional PostgreSQL connection parameters (e.g., options=-c statement_timeout=0) via a crafted Host or Name config value, disabling statement timeouts on fee queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a goroutine leak caused by the cancel function from context.WithTimeout() in GetDbWithContext() never being called by fee query handlers, causing the context to remain open until timeout, holding DB connections and allowing concurrent fee modifications to be observed mid-query?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MaxIdleConnections setting in ConnectToDb() to force the pool to maintain connections in an idle state that bypasses PostgreSQL's idle_in_transaction_session_timeout, allowing fee-related transactions to remain open and observe uncommitted fee modifications?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() uses gorm.Open() with a gormlogrus logger that may log full SQL queries including fee values, causing ChargedTxFee data to be exposed in logs that an attacker can read to understand fee structures and craft fee manipulation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection string validation in ConnectToDb() to cause the DB to connect to a replica with replication lag, causing fee queries to return ChargedTxFee values from before a fee schedule update, effectively serving stale fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the statementTimeout unit conversion (seconds) in GetDbWithContext() by providing a statementTimeout of 1, causing fee queries that span multiple DB pages to be cancelled mid-read and return partial ChargedTxFee results that do not reflect the complete fee state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool configuration in ConnectToDb() (MaxOpenConnections, MaxIdleConnections) to create a connection starvation scenario where fee queries are forced to reuse connections with dirty read states, returning ChargedTxFee values modified outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not set a PostgreSQL transaction isolation level, causing fee queries to run at the default READ COMMITTED level and observe intermediate fee modifications made by concurrent transactions, returning fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection health checks in ConnectToDb() after pool setup, causing the pool to serve broken connections that return error values for ChargedTxFee queries, which may be silently treated as zero fees by upstream callers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDbWithContext() does not enforce read-only transactions on the returned *gorm.DB, allowing a compromised or buggy caller to execute UPDATE statements on the transaction table's ChargedTxFee column through the same DB handle used for reads?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MaxLifetime pool setting in ConnectToDb() set to a very large value, causing connections to persist across fee schedule updates and serve ChargedTxFee values computed under the old fee schedule, violating the design parameter that fees reflect the current schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not validate dbConfig.Pool.MaxOpenConnections for a zero value, causing SetMaxOpenConns(0) to be called which in Go's database/sql means unlimited connections, enabling connection flooding that degrades DB performance and causes fee query inconsistencies?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not set a PostgreSQL search_path, allowing a malicious DB user or schema injection to redirect fee-related table queries (transaction, ChargedTxFee) to attacker-controlled shadow tables with modified fee values?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the context propagation in GetDbWithContext() by sending requests with contexts that carry attacker-controlled deadline values, causing fee queries to be cancelled at precise moments that result in partial fee aggregation results being returned as complete?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of prepared statement caching configuration in ConnectToDb(), causing GORM to use unprepared queries for fee lookups that are vulnerable to query plan cache poisoning, returning incorrect ChargedTxFee values for specific transaction hashes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not configure PostgreSQL application_name, making it impossible to distinguish fee-query connections from other connections in pg_stat_activity, allowing an attacker to monitor and time fee queries to infer fee schedule changes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool's MaxIdleConnections setting in ConnectToDb() to keep connections alive across fee schedule file uploads (transactionResultFeeScheduleFilePartUploaded), causing fee queries on idle connections to return pre-update ChargedTxFee values?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the time.Duration arithmetic in GetDbWithContext() where statementTimeout is multiplied by time.Second, by providing a statementTimeout value of 0 via config, causing the timeout to be exactly 0 nanoseconds and immediately cancelling all fee queries, returning empty fee results?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() uses postgres.Open() without specifying connect_timeout, causing fee query connections to hang indefinitely during network partitions and eventually return stale cached ChargedTxFee values from the pool?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of row-level locking in the DB connection setup in ConnectToDb(), allowing concurrent fee reads and writes to the transaction table to produce non-serializable ChargedTxFee values that violate fee design parameters?
